summary: react-simple-utils-kit exfiltrates Coze API credentials to attacker-controlled host on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js script reads installer-side environment variables (COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN, COZE_LOOP_API_TOKEN, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, TOKEN_ENDPOINT, SPACE_ID), performs an OAuth token exchange, brute-force enumerates Coze API endpoints with the obtained bearer token, and POSTs the harvested credentials and API responses over plain HTTP to http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (a requestrepo.com collector). The script (postinstall.js:7) hardcodes the exfil endpoint as `BURL = 'http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com'` and uses execSync to run curl commands with unsanitized env-var values, providing an additional shell-injection surface on the installer host. The package's advertised purpose is a date-formatting utility (index.js exports formatDate/formatDateTime) — entirely unrelated to the postinstall behavior, with a generic React-utility name acting as a cover story. This is a credential-harvest supply-chain attack that fires automatically on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
